title: taraxacum pervalidum
intro: Taraxacum pervalidum, also known as the common dandelion, is a perennial plant that belongs to the sunflower family. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its rich content of vitamins A, C, and K, as well as minerals like potassium and iron. The medicinal properties of Taraxacum pervalidum include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and lower blood pressure, making it beneficial for liver and kidney health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or capsules, and are often used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
